channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The big question going into NBA All-Star weekend: Will the new format work? There is some skepticism
item.description: Red Auerbach once got ejected from an All-Star Game for arguing a call. Rick Barry and Bob Cousy each fouled out of All-Star Games twice, the only players who can say that. Dwyane Wade was whistled for the first flagrant All-Star foul after breaking Kobe Bryant’s nose.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anthony Davis is out at least through All-Star break after injuring groin in Mavs debut
item.description: Anthony Davis is out at least through the All-Star break after sustaining a groin injury in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks following the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brewers unveil patch they will wear on their uniforms to honor Bob Uecker
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — The Milwaukee Brewers have unveiled a patch they will wear on their uniform this season to honor longtime broadcaster Bob Uecker, who died last month. The patch will appear on the sleeve of the Brewers’ uniforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here are the top 5 commercials from the Super Bowl
item.description: some people may not have been watching this year’s Super Bowl just for the football or rap superstar Kendrick Lamar’s mid-game performance.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl losers' merch passed to other nations
item.description: The NFL plans for both outcomes so they're ready regardless of who wins. What that inevitably means is a whole lot of merchandise that's pre-printed and pre-made – but fans won't ever be able to buy it.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Chiefs for Super Bowl win, praising Mahomes
item.description: “I guess, you have to say that when a quarterback wins as much as he’s won, I have to go with Kansas City,” Trump told Fox News’s Bret Baier.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Clippers dominate fourth quarter in win over Jazz
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - The Los Angeles Clippers broke open a close game in the fourth quarter, outscoring the Jazz 36-24, en route to a 130-110 win over Utah Saturday night. Jordan Clarkson led the Jazz with 24 points, while Keyonte George added 21 off the bench.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Padres sign outfielder/first baseman Connor Joe for 2025 season
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made a move to bolster their roster for the upcoming season by signing outfielder and first baseman Connor Joe to a one-year contract, President of Baseball Operations and General Manager A.J. Preller announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: The well-liked Jauron moved into coaching and five years later was hired by the Bills as a defensive backs coach.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CMU Football signing day recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The football program is entering a new era after 42 student-athletes officially committed to the Mavericks, officials said. “The thing I tell these guys when they go through recruiting is, ‘You’ve got to be where your feet are.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Martinez scores 30 as USU beats Fresno State, 89-81
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Ian Martinez scored 30 points and led Utah State to an 89-81 victory over Fresno State Friday night. Martinez, who made 10 of 14 shots from the field, tied a career high with eight rebounds for the Aggies (21-3, 11-2 Mountain West Conference).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks take losing streak into matchup with the Predators
item.description: Nashville Predators (18-27-7, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (16-31-6, in the Central Division) Chicago; Friday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks play the Nashville Predators after losing three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz acquire guard KJ Martin at trade deadline
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a busy trade deadline for the Utah Jazz, but when all the dust settled, the Jazz are coming away with one new player and five draft picks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Madsen brothers lead Utes past Colorado, 72-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Lawson Lovering scored 12 points and Utah kept Colorado winless in the Big 12 beating the Buffaloes 72-59 on Wednesday night. Ezra Ausar and reserve Mason Madsen each scored 10 points for the Utes (13-9, 5-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Several student-athletes lock in colleges on first NSD of 2025
item.description: Sean Lewis made it clear he wanted to put a fence around San Diego in the recruiting world. He proved it in the high school realm by bringing in 2024 Iron Hog winner Jett Thomas of La Jolla.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs hire former USF head coach Charlie Strong as defensive line coach
item.description: According to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport, the Bucs have hired former USF head coach Charlie Strong to be their new defensive line coach.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Influencers, retired Bay Area sports stars headline NBA All-Star Celebrity Game in Oakland
item.description: (KRON) -- The NBA released the list of celebrities that will take the floor in Oakland for the NBA All-Star Celebrity Game on Feb. 14. A combination of influencers, retired athletes, film stars and more will put their basketball skills to test.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Former NFL player Rashard Cook looks to shape next generation at Mater Dei
item.description: "We had great experiences past high school, and I want to recreate that for the guys that want that experience.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5
